summary: Surface.Canvas.TotalMatrix always returns identity matrix on SKGLView (macOS, OpenGL-backed surface), while SKCanvasView correctly reflects pan/pinch gesture transforms applied to the canvas.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Surface.Canvas.TotalMatrix always returns identity matrix on SKGLView

stepsToReproduce: Use SKGLView in a Xamarin.Forms app on macOS
Apply pan/pinch gestures that modify the canvas matrix
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 1.68.2-preview.45, Visual Studio for Mac 8.5, Xamarin.Forms, macOS 10.15.3, Intel Iris Plus Graphics 655

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The macOS SKGLView implementation still uses SKAutoCanvasRestore wrapping OnPaintSurface; no evidence of a fix in current source.

analysis.summary: The macOS SKGLView (OpenGL-backed) canvas is GPU-backed via GRContext/GRBackendRenderTarget. The canvas is persistent and reused across frames with SKAutoCanvasRestore saving/restoring state per frame. The TotalMatrix property reads back the current CTM via sk_canvas_get_matrix. For GPU-backed surfaces created with GRSurfaceOrigin.BottomLeft, Skia may handle coordinate origin internally rather than via the canvas CTM, potentially causing TotalMatrix to report differently from raster (CPU) surfaces. SKCanvasView creates a fresh CPU-backed surface per frame, so user-applied matrices show correctly. The root cause needs confirmation via reproduction.

codeInvestigation.finding: DrawRect uses SKAutoCanvasRestore(canvas, true) around OnPaintSurface invocation; canvas is cached across frames as a field. The canvas originates from SKSurface.Create(context, renderTarget, GRSurfaceOrigin.BottomLeft, colorType).

DrawRect creates a fresh CPU-backed surface each frame via SKCGSurfaceFactory.CreateSurface, then calls OnPaintSurface with no SKAutoCanvasRestore wrapper. User transforms applied in the handler are reflected correctly in TotalMatrix.
TotalMatrix delegates to TotalMatrix44.Matrix which calls sk_canvas_get_matrix via P/Invoke. This returns the current CTM for any canvas type.

keySignals.interpretation: SKCanvasView correctly reflects applied transforms in TotalMatrix
GPU-backed canvas TotalMatrix always returns identity regardless of gesture transforms applied

analysis.rationale: This is a type/bug in area/SkiaSharp.Views: TotalMatrix returns identity on OpenGL GPU-backed canvases while identical transforms show correctly on CPU-backed canvases. The issue is confined to the macOS OpenGL backend. The area is SkiaSharp.Views (not SkiaSharp core) because the difference in behavior relates to how the GL view wraps and reuses the GPU canvas. Platform is os/macOS since the reporter tested only on macOS. Backend is backend/OpenGL given SKGLView uses OpenGL.
nextQuestions: Does the user apply the gesture matrix explicitly inside PaintSurface, or does the user expect the canvas transform to persist from a previous gesture event outside PaintSurface?
Does the bug reproduce on the current main branch?
Does iOS SKGLView show the same TotalMatrix identity behavior?
Is TotalMatrix wrong only when transforms are applied via SetMatrix, or also via Concat/Scale/Translate?

resolution.hypothesis: SKAutoCanvasRestore resets the canvas CTM to identity at the start of each DrawRect frame. If the user is applying the gesture matrix outside PaintSurface (expecting it to persist), or if the GPU canvas CTM isn't being propagated by sk_canvas_get_matrix as expected, TotalMatrix would always read as identity.

proposals.title: Reproduce and investigate TotalMatrix on GPU-backed canvas
proposals.description: Create a minimal repro that applies a transform via canvas.SetMatrix() inside PaintSurface of SKGLView and reads TotalMatrix. Compare behavior with SKCanvasView. Check if the issue is in how sk_canvas_get_matrix works with GRSurfaceOrigin.BottomLeft.

proposals.title: Store and re-apply transform matrix each frame
proposals.description: Store the accumulated gesture matrix in a field and explicitly call canvas.SetMatrix(gestureMatrix) at the start of each PaintSurface invocation, because SKAutoCanvasRestore resets the canvas state after each frame in SKGLView.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Reproduce and investigate TotalMatrix on GPU-backed canvas
resolution.recommendedReason: The root cause isn't fully confirmed — whether TotalMatrix reads wrong or the canvas transform isn't being applied at all needs determination before a fix can be written.

actionability.reason: Repro code is partial (no minimal runnable sample), but the observed output clearly demonstrates wrong behavior. The issue needs reproduction on current code to confirm it still exists and identify the root cause.

actions.description: Ask for minimal repro and clarify how the matrix is applied; provide workaround
